pr.title: fix: add Yahoo Finance retry/back-off and TTL cache to resolve rate-limit errors
pr.body: ## Problem Users were seeing **"API 請求過於頻繁（00631L.TW）"** errors because: 1. `/api/portfolio/prices` called `yf.Ticker(symbol).info` in a tight loop with **zero delay** between symbols — easily triggering Yahoo Finance's 429 limit for portfolios with several holdings. 2. `_analyze_one()` in the advisor agent had a 1 s inter-stock delay but **no retry logic** — a single transient 429 immediately produced the rate-limit error message with no recovery attempt. 3. No shared cache existed, so the advisor pipeline and the prices endpoint each independently fetched the same symbol's `.info` data, doubling the request count. ## Solution ### New file: `utils/yfinance_utils.py` | Feature | Detail | |---|---| | `is_rate_limit_error(exc)` | Detects 429 / rate-limit exceptions by keyword matching | | `yf_info_with_retry()` | Fetches `Ticker.info` with up to 3 retries; delays: 5 s → 10 s → 20 s (exponential back-off) | | `yf_history_with_retry()` | Same back-off for `Ticker.history()` | | 5-min TTL in-memory cache | `.info` results are cached keyed by symbol; advisor agent and prices endpoint share the same cache — avoids duplicate HTTP requests | ### `agents/holding_advisor_agent.py` - Replace bare `yf.Ticker` calls with `yf_info_with_retry` / `yf_history_with_retry` - Refactor `_yahoo_price_and_bar()` to accept a plain `dict` instead of a `Ticker` object - Increase `REQUEST_DELAY` from **1.0 s → 1.5 s** between stocks - Use `is_rate_limit_error()` in the except handler; error message now accurately states that auto-retry was already attempted before showing the failure ### `agents/tw_market_agent.py` - Use `yf_history_with_retry()` in `build_taiwan_market_snapshot()` ### `app.py` — `/api/portfolio/prices` - Use `yf_info_with_retry()` (cache + retry) instead of bare `yf.Ticker().info` - Add **0.5 s inter-symbol delay** to spread requests - Log rate-limit failures distinctly from other errors ## Expected outcome - Transient Yahoo Finance 429 errors are silently retried with back-off; users only see the error message if all 3 retries fail - The prices endpoint no longer fires burst requests for multi-symbol portfolios - If the advisor runs within 5 minutes of the prices fetch, `.info` comes from cache — halving the effective request count
pr.author: PorscheWen
